things.name: Les Machines de l'Ile
things.description: Jules Verne-inspired mechanical art installation with a giant elephant you can ride

things.name: Chateau des Ducs de Bretagne
things.description: 15th-century castle and moat that was the seat of the Dukes of Brittany - free to enter

things.name: Passage Pommeraye
things.description: Magnificent 1843 three-level shopping arcade - the finest example of its era in France

things.name: Musee d'Arts de Nantes
things.description: Excellent fine arts museum housed in a stunning neoclassical palace with a modern extension

things.name: Cathedral Saint-Pierre-et-Saint-Paul
things.description: Magnificent Gothic cathedral with Flemish Renaissance tomb of Francis II

things.name: Ile de Nantes
things.description: Former shipyard island transformed into a creative district with art, restaurants and riverside walks

things.name: Le Voyage a Nantes Art Trail
things.description: Permanent outdoor art trail with a green line on the pavement connecting 30+ works

things.name: Natural History Museum
things.description: Natural history museum in a neoclassical palace with a famous collection of 750,000 specimens

things.name: Jardin des Plantes
things.description: One of France's finest botanical gardens with exotic plants and artistic topiary

things.name: Lieu Unique
things.description: LU biscuit factory converted into Nantes' national arts centre with bar, hammam and terrace
